data.title: Canada announces new chief trade negotiator to the United States
Eli Lilly and Company Announces Positive Topline Results from the Phase 3 LIBRETTO-432 Clinical Trial of Retevmo
data.description:
Eli Lilly and Company announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 LIBRETTO-432 clinical trial of Retevmo (selpercatinib) as adjuvant therapy versus placebo. The study met its primary endpoint, demonstrating a highly statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in investigator-assessed event-free survival (EFS) in patients with early-stage (II-IIIA) revised during transfection (RET) fusion-positive non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Overall survival results trended in favor of selpercatinib, but were immature at the time of this analysis with few events observed.

The overall safety profile of selpercatinIB in LIBRETTO-432 was generally consistent with previously reported trials in the selpercatinib development program. Det Detailed results will be presented at an upcoming medical congress, submitted to a peer-reviewed journal, and discussed with health authorities globally. LIBRETTO-432 is the first and only randomized Phase 3 study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of a selective RET kinase inhibitor as adjuvant therapy in this population.

NSCLC accounts for about 85% of all lung cancer diagnoses in the U.S., and around 30% of patients with NSCLC present with stage IB-IIIA disease. Approximately 50% of people with NSCLC have actionable biomarkers, and RET fusions have been identified in one to two% of all NSCLC cases. About LIBRETTO-432 is a Phase 3, global, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, controlled clinical trial of RET fusion-positive NSCLC following completion of definitive radiotherapy or surgery with curative intent, and other adjuvant therapy, if indicated.

The trial enrolled 151 patients who were randomized 1:1 to receive either selpercatinib or placebo as adjuvant therapy for RET fusion-positive NSCL C. RET-driver alterations are predominantly mutually exclusive from other oncogenic drivers. Retevmo is a U.S. FDA-approved oral prescription medicine, 120 mg or 160 mg dependent on weight (IMPORTANT SAFETY information for RET fusion-positive lung cancer). Hepatotoxicity: Serious hepatic adverse reactions occurred in 3% of patients treated with Retevmo.

Avoid coadministration of Retevmo with P-gp or BCRP substrates where minimal concentration changes may lead to increased adverse reactions. If coadministration cannot be avoided, follow recommendations for P-gp and BCRP substrates provided in their approved product labeling. No dosage modification is recommended for patients with mild to severe renal impairment (estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate [eGFR] 15 to 89 mL/min, estimated by Modification of Diet in Renal Disease [MDRD] equation).

A recommended dosage has not been established for patients with end-stage renal disease. With each step toward a healthier world, the company're motivated by one thing: making life better for millions more people. That includes delivering innovative clinical trials that reflect the diversity of world and working to ensure its medicines are accessible and affordable.

data.title: United Airlines Selects GE Aerospace GEnx Engines to Power New 787 Dreamliners
data.description:
GE Aerospace announced that United Airlines has selected 300 GEnx engines to power their new Boeing 787 Dreamliners. The agreement also includes additional spare engines and brings United Airlines' 787 fleet to more than 200 GEnx powered aircraft. Engineered with advanced materials and cutting-edge technologies, the GEnx engine family delivers greater durability and time on wing for customers.

Combined with a 99.98 dispatch rate GEnx is also highly reliable. GE Aerospace's relationship with United Airlines began in 1968. The carrier currently operates an extensive fleet of GE Aerospace and CFM powered aircraft that includes CF6, GE90, GEnx, CFM56-7B, LEAP and CF34 engines.

data.title: Volkswagen plans to cut costs by 20% by end 2028: media report
data.description:
STORY: Volkswagen plans to cut costs by a fifth across all brands by the end of 2028.

That's according to a report in Manager Magazin on Monday (February 16).

The report said CEO Oliver Blume and finance chief Arno Antlitz presented a "massive" savings plan at a closed-door meeting with top executives in Berlin last month.

Monday's report added it wasn't clear at the meeting where the savings are due to be made.

Or where cooperation between the brands is to be improved

But it did say plant closures could be considered.

VW's works council chief, Daniela Cavallo, acknowledged the report and pointed to a deal struck with Volkswagen AG at the end of 2024.

They agreed on measures for competitiveness and for a socially acceptable impact on the workforce.

She added - quote - "with this agreement, we have expressly ruled out plant closures and layoffs for operational reasons."

The German auto giant wants to strengthen finances to deal with the impact of higher costs, a tough Chinese market and U.S. tariffs.

Europe's largest carmaker is in the process of cutting 35,000 jobs in Germany by 2030.

Its core brand announced in January it planned to reduce management positions and consolidate its production platform.

It aims to save $1.2 billion by the start of the next decade.

Today's Stock in Europe - Kone literally lifted by Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Kone shares (+6.77% to €45.9) are climbing on the Helsinki Stock Exchange, buoyed by Deutsche Bank's comments, which upgraded the lift and escalator maker from "hold" to "buy", viewing the stock's recent underperformance as an attractive entry point.

In a note published this morning, Deutsche Bank said it expects an acceleration in the Nordic industrial group's growth in 2026 and 2027, prompting it to raise its price target to €71 (from €62).

In light of the pullback that followed the presentation of annual results earlier this month, the German bank said it sees an interesting entry point in the stock.

"The group reported solid growth, with the exception of China, a country whose headwinds are weighing less and less in the business 'mix'," the institution noted in its report.

In its opinion, the structural trend linked to equipment modernisation should allow it to generate average annual revenue growth of more than 15% in the coming years, excluding the maintenance services business, where growth is also expected to pick up to reach 7% in 2027, according to its forecasts.

"Compared with its competitors, Kone is better positioned to benefit from building modernisation, thanks to its scale and its strategy of focusing on partial renovations rather than large-scale projects," DB added.

Kone, Europe's 100th-largest market capitalisation with a valuation of around €28bn, said on February 6 that its Q4 order intake surprised pleasantly, although its outlook for FY 2026 somewhat disappointed the market.

For 2026, the group is forecasting revenue growth of between 2% and 6% at constant exchange rates, implying a midpoint of 4%, below the 5.5% consensus, with an adjusted operating margin (Ebit) of between 12.3% and 13%, again below the 12.9% consensus.

The announcement was followed by a 6.5% drop in the stock on the Nasdaq OMX Helsinki market in Finland over the next two sessions, a pullback that continued through last Friday.

Operating in more than 60 countries, Kone generated revenue of €11.2bn in 2025 and employs nearly 60,000 people worldwide.

In France, Belgium and Luxembourg, the group - which equips, among others, the St-Denis-Pleyel station, the Val Thorens ski resort and the Opéra Garnier - has around 3,400 employees.

data.title: Generali Continues Buyback, Acquires Shares Worth Over EUR54 Million
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Assicurazioni Generali Spa announced on Monday that it acquired nearly 1.6 million of its own ordinary shares between February 9 and 13.

The shares were purchased at an average unit price of EUR34.92, for a total value of EUR54.4 million.

Following these purchases, the company now holds a total of 50.5 million treasury shares, equivalent to approximately 3.3% of its share capital.

Generali shares are up 0.9% at EUR35.14 each.

By Antonio Di Giorgio, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

Hong Kong Court dismisses 2024 petition to wind up Country Garden
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - Chinese property developer Country Garden said on Monday the Hong Kong High Court has dismissed the winding-up petition filed by Ever Credit against the property developer in 2024.

Ever Credit Ltd, a unit of Hong Kong-listed Kingboard Holdings, filed the petition in February 2024 against Country Garden for non-payment of a $205 million loan.

The petition was filed a month after the now delisted China Evergrande Group, once one of China's top-selling property developers, was ordered to be liquidated by a Hong Kong court in a blow to Beijing's efforts to restore confidence in China's property sector.

The court had previously adjourned the hearing on the winding-up petition at least five times since 2024.

The slump in China's property sector is now in its fifth year. The sector, which once made up a quarter of China's gross domestic product, has been hit by slowing demand, with homebuyer sentiment also hurt by developers' defaults.

(Reporting by Sherin Sunny in Bengaluru; Editing by Jan Harvey and Susan Fenton)

data.title: Trump's new ambassador Bozell arrives in South Africa amid strained ties
Oddo BHF raises TP for AstraZeneca shares
data.description:
The broker believes that the Swedish-British drugmaker is starting 2026 with an attractive growth profile through to 2030. Its strength, analysts say, is that it can "invest heavily in its pipeline while continuing to increase profitability". They add that "this financial discipline, combined with remarkable commercial execution (an attractive product mix today that offsets the expected pressure on Farxiga), remains a major differentiating factor". The treatment, originally for type 2 diabetes and since used for heart failure and chronic kidney disease, is set to enter a period of intense competitive and regulatory pressure as patents expire.

Oddo BHFexpects a clinically busy year ahead in 2026, which will enable the company not to be dependent on a single result in both the short and long term.

Bridgestone Corporation authorizes a Buyback Plan.
data.description: The Board of Directors of Bridgestone Corporation has authorized a buyback plan on February 16, 2026.

data.title: Bridgestone Corporation announces an Equity Buyback for 60,000,000 shares, representing 4.7% for ¥150,000 million.
data.description:
Bridgestone Corporation (TSE:5108) announces a share repurchase program. Under the program, the company will repurchase 60,000,000 shares, representing 4.7% of its share capital, for ¥150,000 million. The purpose of the program is enhancing enterprise value, and to implement measures for an optimal capital structure (improving capital efficiency).

Repurchased shares will be cancelled. The program will run until August 31, 2026. As of January 31, 2026, the company had 1,276,571,421 shares outstanding (excluding treasury shares) and 57,465,621 shares in treasury.

Siemens Hits Yearly Low Amid AI Fears
data.description:
FRANKFURT (dpa-AFX) - After recently reaching record highs, Siemens shares fell sharply at the start of the week due to mounting concerns over artificial intelligence (AI). By Monday afternoon, the stock had dropped to its lowest level this year, last trading down 5.6 percent at €236.95. Last Thursday, Siemens had reached a peak of €275.75. Meanwhile, in Paris, Dassault Systèmes shares plunged 8.8 percent, hitting their lowest point since August 2017.

According to market observers, investors are increasingly worried that industrial software could be threatened by advanced AI tools. There is concern that software used to create digital twins—virtual replicas of physical objects—could be displaced by AI. Analysts note that the practice of buying all solutions from a single provider, such as Dassault Systèmes or Siemens, is becoming less justifiable as AI now allows companies to combine their software with "open" systems.

Société Générale Benefits from Favorable Comments by Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Société Générale is up 3%, placing it among the top performers on the CAC 40, buoyed by favorable comments from Goldman Sachs, which has reiterated its buy recommendation and raised its 12-month price target by approximately 15% to 84 EUR.

The broker has increased its EPS estimates by an average of 4% for 2026-2028, mainly due to revenue assumptions being raised by 1 to 2% over the period, against a backdrop of better-than-expected trends in the fourth quarter and upgraded management targets.

Additionally, Goldman Sachs has now incorporated its 2027 estimates at 100% in its valuation (up from 75% previously), using a higher target P/E multiple of 9 times, compared to the previous 8.5 times.

"The higher target multiple reflects our constructive view of the operational plan, which we see as prudent and achievable, supported by efficiency gains for the group," the intermediary stated, thus raising its price target accordingly.

Safran in the stratosphere, but perhaps on the edge of an air pocket
data.description:
A star of the French market and the holder of unique, non-replicable and sovereign expertise, Safran has also benefited from Europe's defence boom - a segment that represents 40% of its revenue - and from order volumes that remain strong in civil aviation. 

As usual, MarketScreener steps back to place the annual results - widely discussed in the business press - in a longer-term perspective, namely over the long cycle from 2010 to 2025. 

It shows that Safran's revenue has almost tripled over the period, from €11bn to €31bn, and that operating profit has almost been multiplied by five.

Regarding profit before tax and exceptional items - subject to significant distortions, as evidenced by an FX loss of €5.2bn last year and an FX gain of €5.8bn this year - it has been multiplied by fifty. All of this, while the number of shares outstanding has remained stable.

Value creation was therefore spectacular in every respect, and all the more admirable because this expansion was entirely self-financed, while the group has always maintained an extremely robust capital structure, with negligible leverage ratios.

The group has not forgotten to reward its shareholders over the period either. Beyond a share price multiplied by 20 since 2010, dividend distributions have increased tenfold.

Safran's market valuation does full justice to this exceptional run. However, it too is at record highs, both in terms of multiples of its earnings and of its shareholders' equity.

These stratospheric levels inevitably raise questions. For sure, the slightest setback will now be snarled at by investors.

data.title: L'Oréal Rebounds and Leads the CAC, Disappointing Results Quickly Forgotten
data.description:
L'Oréal managed to regain momentum on Monday after its sharp fall on Friday, buoyed by reassuring analyst comments that helped put the French cosmetics giant's Q4 2025 underperformance into perspective.

The stock rebounded by 3.3%, marking the strongest rise on the CAC 40 index and one of the biggest gains on the pan-European STOXX Europe 600.

In doing so, the share erased a significant portion of its fall of nearly 5% on Friday, following the announcement of "only" 6% organic growth in Q4—just short of analysts' expectations of 6.3%, and below the "unofficial" consensus of 7%.

This disappointment was mainly linked to a slowdown in growth for its luxury products (Lancôme, Yves Saint Laurent, Aesop, and Maison Margiela) and the persistent weakness of its business in China, where the environment remains challenging, even if there are signs of slight improvement.

"The debate mainly focuses on the pace of recovery in China, where the fourth quarter disappointed but the first quarter appears to have started well," Deutsche Bank analysts noted this morning.

"It will likely be necessary to wait for first-quarter results from the major luxury players to truly understand how the situation will evolve," the German bank added.

Beyond the disappointing Q4 results, many investors prefer to focus on the encouraging signs of a rapid improvement in the beauty specialist's performance, anticipating a certain revaluation of the stock as these temporary adverse factors recede.

While UBS teams lament a "missed opportunity" with the latest results presentation, they also assure that the "dust should settle quickly."

"Rather than significantly outperforming expectations and convincingly demonstrating it had turned a corner—with an acceleration in growth—L'Oréal found itself caught by unfavorable one-off factors (notably unexpected destocking in Asia's 'travel retail' sector or the timing gap between deliveries and final sales in the United States), which overshadowed the company's underlying progress," commented Guillaume Delmas, UBS equity specialist, this morning.

He says that the market should soon set aside the disappointing results of the past two quarters and focus more on the numerous strengths that define the company.

In its research note, amongst other things, UBS highlights: (1) the group's broad-based improvement in like-for-like growth, (2) a recovery in its main growth drivers, (3) a strong start to the year supporting the group's ambition to accelerate growth in 2026, (4) the prospect of numerous acquisitions offering synergy opportunities, (5) its pioneering status in AI among leading beauty sector stocks, positioning it as a clear winner in the ongoing technological revolution, and (6) an attractive valuation.

While the stock currently trades at a 2026 P/E of 27.5x—a 35% premium to the broader European consumer staples sector (20x)—UBS notes that its P/E remains well below its ten-year average of 30.5x.

Following today's rise, the share is now up 3.3% year-to-date, compared with a 2.2% gain for the CAC 40.

Over the past 12 months, it is still up 11.5%, versus a 1.9% increase for the CAC.

In technical terms, L'Oréal is poised to fill its "pullback" below the €385 threshold that followed Thursday evening's results release and remains comfortably above its 100- and 200-day moving averages, at €373.5 and €376 respectively, signaling a still-solid stock market momentum.
